# [clang-fuzzer] `clang::MemberPointerType::isSugared() const`: Assertion `!D1 == !D2' failed.

**Author:** gal1ium
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/165997
**Status:** Closed
**Labels:** clang:frontend, crash-on-invalid, confirmed, generated by fuzzer, regression:21
**Closed Date:** 2026-02-28T23:28:25Z

## Body

Hi, while testing clang by the fuzzing driver `clang-fuzzer` in 531fd45e9238d0485e3268aaf14ae15d01c7740f, it found a crashing case:

PoC:
```c
truct S{S&ntity(;template<typename T,typename U>oid Call(T,U){Call(&::ntity,0)
```

Based on https://godbolt.org/z/rfsTT5Y6v it only happens in clang-21.1.0:
```
clang++: /root/llvm-project/llvm/tools/clang/lib/AST/Type.cpp:5430: bool clang::MemberPointerType::isSugared() const: Assertion `!D1 == !D2' failed.
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/ and include the crash backtrace, preprocessed source, and associated run script.
Stack dump:
0.	Program arguments: /opt/compiler-explorer/clang-assertions-trunk/bin/clang++ -g -o /app/output.s -mllvm --x86-asm-syntax=intel -fno-verbose-asm -S --gcc-toolchain=/opt/compiler-explorer/gcc-snapshot -fcolor-diagnostics -fno-crash-diagnostics <source>
1.	<source>:1:78: current parser token ')'
2.	<source>:1:1: parsing struct/union/class body 'S'
3.	<source>:1:62: parsing function body 'S::Call'
4.	<source>:1:62: in compound statement ('{}')
```

<details>
Hi, while testing clang by the fuzzing driver `clang-fuzzer` in 531fd45e9238d0485e3268aaf14ae15d01c7740f, it found a crashing case:

PoC:
```c
truct S{S&amp;ntity(;template&lt;typename T,typename U&gt;oid Call(T,U){Call(&amp;::ntity,0)
```

Based on https://godbolt.org/z/rfsTT5Y6v it only happens in clang-21.1.0:

### Comment 3 - Moksha25-tech

Hi, I’m starting to look into this issue.

I noticed that this assertion (clang::MemberPointerType::isSugared()) appears to be the same one triggering the newer report in #175783, although with a different reproducer and stack dump. Since both seem to involve invalid member pointer construction during error handling, they may share the same root cause.

I’ll try to reproduce both cases locally and investigate whether a single fix can address them simultaneously. I’ll update here with any findings.
